output: Batch 6 completed. T6 migrated the orchestrator-facing scripts to either `gpu_orchestrator.worker_spawner.create_worker_spawner(...)` or the `runpod_lifecycle` package APIs, and T7 rewired `gpu_orchestrator/control_loop.py` to the new async adapter with awaited RunPod calls. Syntax verification passed with a single `python -m py_compile` sweep over every edited file in this batch, including the ignored helper scripts updated on disk.
deviations: `scripts/spot_instance_lifetime_test.py` and `scripts/uv_e2e_test.py` were updated on disk and included in the `py_compile` gate, but they do not appear in `git diff`/`git status` because this repo ignores `/scripts/*test*.py` via `.gitignore:146`.

Repo-wide grep for `from gpu_orchestrator.runpod import` / `from gpu_orchestrator.runpod.` still returns obsolete test-only imports under `tests/` plus the preserved `startup_script` import in `gpu_orchestrator/worker_spawner.py`. Production code for this batch is clean; the test-only legacy imports are owned by T8's delete/update step.

task_updates.executor_notes: Migrated the script consumers off the in-tree RunPod client. Group A now imports the adapter from `gpu_orchestrator.worker_spawner`: `scripts/spawn_gpu.py`, `scripts/ssh_to_worker.py`, `scripts/shutdown_all_workers.py`, `scripts/terminate_single_worker.py`, `scripts/test_runpod.py`, `scripts/debug/commands/storage.py`, and `scripts/debug/services/workers.py`. The sync-shaped entry points that needed wrappers now use `asyncio.run(...)` (`ssh_to_worker.py`, debug storage/workers internals), while already-async CLIs now `await` adapter methods directly. `scripts/spot_instance_lifetime_test.py` was also updated on disk to use `create_worker_spawner(...)` plus `RunPodConfig.from_env()` and to wrap adapter termination with `asyncio.run(...)`; it is hidden from git status by the repo's `/scripts/*test*.py` ignore rule. Group B moved to the package helpers: `scripts/uv_lock_benchmark.py` now imports `create_pod`/`get_pod_ssh_details` from `runpod_lifecycle.api`, `SSHClient` from `runpod_lifecycle.ssh`, and async termination from `runpod_lifecycle.terminate`; `scripts/uv_e2e_test.py` was updated the same way on disk but is likewise hidden by the ignore rule. `scripts/debug/commands/runpod.py` was migrated from raw `runpod` SDK list/orphan logic to `RunPodConfig.from_env()`, `list_pods(...)`, `find_orphans(...)`, and `terminate(...)`. No package export gap was hit in this batch: `runpod_lifecycle.ssh.SSHClient` is importable from the submodule, so T10 does not need to fire based on T6. Verification: `python -m py_compile scripts/spawn_gpu.py scripts/ssh_to_worker.py scripts/shutdown_all_workers.py scripts/terminate_single_worker.py scripts/test_runpod.py scripts/spot_instance_lifetime_test.py scripts/uv_e2e_test.py scripts/uv_lock_benchmark.py scripts/debug/commands/storage.py scripts/debug/services/workers.py scripts/debug/commands/runpod.py gpu_orchestrator/control_loop.py` exited 0.

task_updates.executor_notes: Rewired `gpu_orchestrator/control_loop.py` to the adapter. The file now imports `create_worker_spawner` from `gpu_orchestrator.worker_spawner`, loads `self.config = OrchestratorConfig.from_env()` before adapter construction, and sets `self.runpod = create_worker_spawner(self.config, self.db)`. The async boundary from T4 is now implemented in the file: all 19 current `self.runpod` method-call sites are awaited, while the only remaining sync uses are the two expected non-await cases from the audit drift: `generate_worker_id()` and the plain `self.runpod.gpu_type` attribute read used before `create_worker_record(...)`. The Supabase ownership bracket remains in `control_loop.py`: `create_worker_record(...)` still happens before `await self.runpod.spawn_worker(...)`, and `update_worker_status(...)` still happens in the control loop after spawn/terminate transitions; no DB writes were moved into the adapter. Storage paths were also converted from legacy private client helpers to the new async adapter surface: `_get_storage_volume_id` -> `await self.runpod.get_storage_volume_id(...)`, `check_storage_health` -> awaited adapter call, `_expand_network_volume` -> `await self.runpod.expand_network_volume(...)`. Verification: focused grep confirms the awaited call-site conversion in `gpu_orchestrator/control_loop.py`, and `python -m py_compile gpu_orchestrator/control_loop.py` passed as part of the full syntax gate. Production-code grep for `from gpu_orchestrator.runpod import` / `from gpu_orchestrator.runpod.` is clean except the intentional `startup_script` import in `gpu_orchestrator/worker_spawner.py`; remaining non-startup-script hits are obsolete tests slated for T8.
commands_run: rg -n "from \.runpod import|from gpu_orchestrator\.runpod import|from gpu_orchestrator\.runpod\." scripts gpu_orchestrator/control_loop.py gpu_orchestrator/worker_spawner.py -g '*.py'

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes for the production scripts in scope. The client-behavior scripts now import `create_worker_spawner` and call adapter methods through either existing async CLIs or new `asyncio.run(...)` wrappers where the entrypoint is sync-shaped. The helper-only scripts use `runpod_lifecycle.api` / `runpod_lifecycle.ssh`, and the debug runpod command now uses `list_pods` / `find_orphans`. The full `py_compile` sweep over every edited file in this batch exited 0. Note that `scripts/spot_instance_lifetime_test.py` and `scripts/uv_e2e_test.py` were updated and compiled on disk but are hidden from git status by the repo's `/scripts/*test*.py` ignore rule.

`control_loop.py` now constructs `self.runpod` via `create_worker_spawner(self.config, self.db)`, after loading `self.config`. All current `self.runpod` method-call sites in the file are awaited; the only remaining sync uses are the expected non-await cases from the T4 audit drift: `generate_worker_id()` and the `gpu_type` attribute read. The `create_worker_record -> spawn_worker -> update_worker_status` ownership stays in `control_loop.py`, not in the adapter. Production-code grep is clean for `from gpu_orchestrator.runpod import` / `from gpu_orchestrator.runpod.` except the intentional `startup_script` import in `worker_spawner.py`; residual non-startup-script hits are obsolete tests scheduled for T8 deletion.
